description: Machine-readable disposition of `vp pm audit --level low` findings for the socialcalc devDependency tree. Regenerate by re-running the audit command below after any dependency change.
scope: All findings are devDependency-only (build/verification tooling). No advisory package appears in the published npm tarball (see package.json "files"); none are reachable from any code shipped to consumers of the socialcalc package.

summary.fixMethod: Narrow package.json "overrides" pinning each package to the lowest already-in-range patched release its real parent(s) already declare compatibility with (no parent's own semver range was violated): @babel/core 7.29.0->7.29.7 (stryker-mutator/instrumenter allows ~7.29.0/^7.28.6), fast-uri 3.1.0->3.1.3 (ajv allows ^3.0.1), qs 6.15.1->6.15.3 (typed-rest-client allows ^6.14.1). Applied via `vp update` after editing the overrides map; verified via `vp install --frozen-lockfile` and `vp pm audit` that no other resolution moved and no peer range was violated.

advisory.title: brace-expansion: Large numeric range defeats documented `max` DoS protection

accepted.ciReachability: Only exercised by `vp exec stryker run` (mutation testing) when minimatch evaluates the static, repo-authored include/exclude glob patterns in stryker's own config. The DoS requires an attacker-controlled brace pattern with an extreme numeric range (e.g. `{1..1e9}`); no user- or network-supplied string ever reaches this code path in this repo. Not present in the published npm tarball (devDependency only). Effective CI reachability: none observed.
accepted.whyNotFixed: brace-expansion also resolves at 2.1.2 via an unrelated, non-vulnerable chain: lemmascript@0.5.13 > ts-morph@25.0.1 > @ts-morph/common@0.26.1 > minimatch@9.0.9 (requires ^2.0.2) > brace-expansion@2.1.2. Bun's package.json "overrides" are top-level-only (no per-parent/nested scoping, unlike npm), so a single override key forces the SAME resolved version onto both chains. Empirically verified: overriding to 5.0.7 collapses the 2.x edge to 5.0.7 too, silently breaking the ^2.0.2 range minimatch@9.0.9 (and therefore @ts-morph/common, used by lemmascript's `lsc gen`/`lsc check`) actually declares. Forcing that cross-major jump on an otherwise non-vulnerable, unrelated edge is the exact kind of breaking/hiding shortcut this remediation pass was told not to take, so the moderate finding on the 5.x edge is left in place instead. No newer minimatch/@stryker-mutator/core release exists yet that self-bumps past 5.0.5 within its own declared range.
accepted.mitigation: None required beyond the existing isolation (dev-only dependency, unreachable from any CI-invoked command, not shipped). No code or config change reduces risk further without the breaking override described above.
accepted.expiryCondition: Re-audit when any of: (a) @stryker-mutator/core or minimatch publish a release pinning brace-expansion >=5.0.6, (b) Bun ships nested/per-parent "overrides" (tracked upstream: oven-sh/bun#6608), letting the 5.x and 2.x edges be pinned independently, or (c) 90 days have elapsed since lastReviewed, whichever is first.

advisory.title: uuid: Missing buffer bounds check in v3/v5/v6 when buf is provided

accepted.dependencyPath: socialcalc (devDependency) > lemmascript@0.5.13 > lemmascript-claimcheck@0.2.0 (peer) > claimcheck@0.6.0 > @anthropic-ai/vertex-sdk@0.14.4 > google-auth-library@9.15.1 > gaxios@6.7.1 (requires ^9.0.1) > uuid@9.0.1
accepted.ciReachability: `lsc` (the lemmascript CLI our scripts call) exposes uuid's vulnerable code only through the separate `lsc claimcheck` subcommand, which forwards to lemmascript-claimcheck's Anthropic Vertex AI backend. This repo's package.json scripts (`verify`, `verify:dafny*`, `verify:lean*`) and .github/workflows/lemmascript.yml invoke only `lsc gen`/`lsc check`/`lsc regen`; `lsc claimcheck` is never invoked by any script or workflow in this repo, and no ANTHROPIC/VERTEX-related call appears in lemmascript's bundled `gen`/`check` code path (verified by string search of node_modules/lemmascript/tools/dist). Effective CI reachability: none — the vulnerable path is present in the tree but dormant.
accepted.whyNotFixed: No 9.x patch exists; the advisory fix is only available from uuid 11.1.1, a major bump. Its immediate parent, gaxios@6.7.1 (the latest gaxios release compatible with google-auth-library@9.15.1), declares `uuid: "^9.0.1"` — a caret range capped below 10.0.0, so 11.1.1 is outside what gaxios itself supports. Checked the whole chain for a compatible upstream bump: google-auth-library's latest (10.9.0) needs gaxios ^7.1.4 (which drops the uuid dependency entirely) and gcp-metadata pinned to 8.1.2; @anthropic-ai/vertex-sdk's latest (0.19.0) needs google-auth-library ^10.2.0; but claimcheck's latest published release (0.6.0, already what we resolve) still pins `@anthropic-ai/vertex-sdk: "^0.14.4"`, so there is no current claimcheck release that would pull any of those newer, uuid-free versions in. Forcing uuid to 11.1.1 (or forcing vertex-sdk/google-auth-library/gaxios past their declared ranges) would be an unreviewed breaking upgrade several layers into a dependency graph we do not own, which this remediation pass was told not to force.
accepted.mitigation: None required today (path is dormant/unreachable). If `lsc claimcheck` is ever adopted in this repo, first re-audit and require lemmascript-claimcheck/claimcheck to have published a release resolving to a uuid-free or patched gaxios chain before wiring it into any script or CI job.
accepted.expiryCondition: Re-audit when any of: (a) the `claimcheck` package publishes a release bumping @anthropic-ai/vertex-sdk to >=0.19.0 (which pulls a uuid-free gaxios/google-auth-library chain), (b) `lsc claimcheck` becomes part of this repo's scripts/CI, or (c) 90 days have elapsed since lastReviewed, whichever is first.
